content:
Are you using "push" in general terms to highlight that emails are not being reguarly synchronised (15 min polling), or do you specifically have an issue with the push service which delivers emails immediatly upon arrival on the server.

For general synchronisation issues, Have you checked the app is excluded from any Android battery optimisations and allowed it to run unrestricted in the background.

  1. Long press Thunderbirds launcher icon.
  2. Click battery usage.
  3. Enable background usage.

The location and name for these settings can vary by phone manufacturer and android version.

Push Service

Android requires the app's push service to run in the foreground and display a permanant notification.

If you close the app using the recent app list, the push service is terminated and won't receive new emails.

If you're using the push service with Android 13 or later, check you've granted the app "Alarm & reminders" permission.

See the guide linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android
creator: platform34

content:
Font controls were deliberately removed from the app. The bug is that they didn't go far enough, with access to the old settings still being reachable via the search field.

The message list user interface is undergoing a significant redesign. The font controls were removed to facilitate this work. Font controls will be reviewed and made available with the new user interface upon released.

You can the developers response about this linked below. https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/9709#issuecomment-3249281014

We’ve removed these settings as part of the ongoing UI redesign, since the previous way of changing font sizes no longer works with the new design system. We’re working on alternative options to bring back some customization in a future update.
creator: platform34

content: Makes no sense! Why remove a useful feature from the current version because it won't work with some new future version still under development?! Remove it from the new version if absolutely necessary, but why alienate users of the current version. They're not gunna wait around for some promised new version if the current version is unusable!
creator: byrnesasylum

content:
Downgrading to v10 where font controls are fully functional is an alternative option available.

All prior versions are available for download from the github link below. V10 is on page 5 https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/releases

To downgrade

  1. Export your settings to file.
  2. Uninstall your current version.
  3. install version 10.
  4. Restore your settings from the export file.
  5. Complete the account authentication.

You can find more information about the plans for the app in the blog and youtube communication linked below.

https://blog.thunderbird.net/2026/02/mobile-progress-report-february-2026/ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j3j96-y-93I
